what_it_does: Defines an unattended, cron-driven coordination protocol in which three named sessions take turns firing every 15 minutes, reading a shared append-only message board and a claims board, each performing one bounded build round, then idling, so that work on a shared codebase can proceed overnight without a human relay.
observations: Uses an append-only, race-safe MESSAGES.md file for inter-session messaging with tagged headers and read-markers.
Staggers crons at :00, :05, :10 in a fixed ring order: cognition -> guided-review -> coherence.
Each fire is one bounded round: read claims and new messages, react, claim one file, build, run company suites green, verify by use on a TEMP store, commit only owned files, release the claim.
If blocked or gated, the session records the blocker, posts a message, and exits without busywork.
Requires each session to maintain an AUTONOMOUS-STATE.md summary for morning review.
Push notification to Tim is reserved for real blockers or destructive calls.

Lists a discipline: registry-is-truth, additive, fail-loud, reflects-never-owns, operator-only floor, verify-by-use, no green-paint, surface-don't-defer, commit-to-main, one driver per shared file via CLAIMS, don't-spin.
A CONVERGENCE-ROUND.md is triggered when all three sessions post lane-complete.
standouts: Replaces Tim's 'go to bed' human relay with a FIRE trigger and session-to-session messaging.
Staggered crons are explicitly designed to let messages propagate and avoid simultaneous commits.
The 'don't-spin' rule: blocked means record + exit, not busywork.
Strict 'NO green-paint' and 'verify-by-use' requirements to prevent unconfirmable progress.
One driver per shared file enforced through a CLAIMS board read before every fire.
conventions: Append-only log with atomic append posting.
Time-sliced cron scheduling with fixed ring order.
Bounded reads and bounded work per cycle.
concerns: Committing directly to main unattended assumes the claims board and staggered timing are sufficient to prevent collisions, but there is no automated enforcement of claims.
Race safety relies on atomic append for messages and manual claim/release discipline; simultaneous claims are not mechanically resolved.
The distinction between 'TEMP store' and 'live' store and the isolation mechanism is not specified.
'PushNotification Tim ONLY if something truly needs him' leaves escalation criteria subjective.
Requires sessions and durable crons to remain running/idle overnight, which is not guaranteed.
notes: This file is the operational runbook for an overnight, three-agent cron-and-message coordination loop.
questions: What exactly is a 'FIRE' and how is it represented in the system?
How are the three sessions created and how is each cron configured as 'durable'?
What is the exact format and location of WORK-SPLIT.md and its CLAIMS section?
What does 'company suites' refer to and how is it invoked?
How is the TEMP store isolated from the live store?
What are the specific steps and criteria in CONVERGENCE-ROUND.md?
How is a PushNotification to Tim implemented?
What is the ledger of 'code/design substrates' this loop is meant to update?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is a protocol for autonomous three-session overnight coordination.
apparent_themes: Verification by real use rather than code inspection
Human escalation only for genuine blockers
Safety through timing and discipline rather than branches
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a reliable, low-risk way for multiple AI sessions to keep building and converging on a single main branch while a human is unavailable, using strict pacing, messaging, and claims to avoid runaway or conflicting changes.
novelty: The 'go to bed' setup: using staggered session crons and an append-only markdown message board to replace a human handoff loop, combined with a one-round-per-fire discipline to prevent unattended runaway work.
contribution: Provides a concrete, time-sliced operational protocol for multi-session coordination, including message tagging, claims-based file locking, bounded build rounds, verification rules, and escalation thresholds that a fused system could keep as a reference runbook.
summary_for_embedding: AUTONOMOUS-LOOP.md defines a cron-paced, message-driven protocol for three sessions to autonomously coordinate overnight work on a shared main branch via an append-only message board and a claims board.
intention_for_embedding: This file was written to let three named sessions continue building and converging without a human relay during unattended hours.
